input.background: A particular random sample of $n$ observations can be used to calculate a sample proportion. The count of successes in the sample will vary according to a probability distribution with parameters $n$ and $p$. When the sample is large, the number of successes will vary according to a Normal distribution with mean and standard deviation. At the same time, the sampling distribution of proportion $\hat{p}$ in large samples will be Normally distributed with mean $p$ and standard deviation.
input.question: What type of probability distribution does the count of successes in the sample follow, and what are its parameters?

meta_info.caption: Fill in the blanks.
meta_info.section: 8.3 SAMPLING BEHAVIOR OF A COUNT AND PROPORTION
